case_number: Criminal Revision Application No. S-64 of 2025
sections_cited: Section 3 of the Illegal Dispossession Act

chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Revision Application No. S-64 of 2025 (Saba Farhat Rashid vs Farhat Rashid and anothers) DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For order on office objection at A 2. For hearing of case 3. For hearing of M.A.No. 3970/2025 4. For hearing of M.A.No. 6306/2025 02.12.2025 Mr. Fahad Ali Hashmi, Advocate for the applicant Ms. Rubina Qadir, Additional Prosecutor General Sindh. -.-.-.-.-.-. Through this Criminal Revision Application, the applicant has assailed the order dated 27.02.2025 passed on an application under Section 3 of the Illegal Dispossession Act, 2005, filed by respondent No.1. The learned trial Court, after receiving the entire file from the Court of Sessions Judge, Karachi South, issued directions to the concerned SHO to conduct the necessary inquiry as required by law and submit a report. However, vide order dated 13.03.2025 passed by this Court in the instant Revision Application, the said order was suspended. After arguing the matter at some length, learned counsel for the applicant does not press the instant Criminal Revision Application and submits that the applicant is ready to join the inquiry, but prays that the SHO as well as the concerned quarters be directed to act strictly in accordance with law. He further submits that the learned trial Court may also be directed to pass a well-reasoned order after hearing the parties. Conversely, the learned Addl:PG has raised no objection to the proposition advanced by learned counsel for the applicant. In view of the above, the concerned SHO as well as any other quarters from whom the trial Court has called or intends to call a report are directed to act strictly within the framework of the Illegal Dispossession Act, 2005.

Conversely, the learned Addl:PG has raised no objection to the proposition advanced by learned counsel for the applicant. In view of the above, the concerned SHO as well as any other quarters from whom the trial Court has called or intends to call a report are directed to act strictly within the framework of the Illegal Dispossession Act, 2005. During the inquiry, they shall afford the applicant an opportunity of hearing and thereafter prepare a report on the basis of the material collected during such inquiry. Learned counsel for the applicant shall be at liberty to address the trial Court on all grounds taken in this Criminal Revision Application, and the learned trial Court, after perusal of the entire material and hearing the parties, shall pass a speaking order. The instant Criminal Revision Application stands disposed of along with listed applications. JUDGE
